reasoning: For short-horizon dates (May 4, May 27), the stock faces negative momentum following a steep drop from ~$60.00 to $54.62 during April, likely caused by apprehension ahead of the May 8 Q1 earnings report (last year the stock fell ~10% around this time). This pushes the short-term probability below 0.5. For the longer horizons (July 26, October 24), Molson Coors' low valuation (P/E ~11.3, solid free cash flow) offers a strong price floor and standard, positive equity drift begins to dominate. Incorporating the instruction that uncertainty should increase and distant probabilities should converge toward 0.5, the probability curve gradually rises from the pre-earnings momentum dip back to a coin-toss.
